mainEntity.name: What is an MBA in Finance?

acceptedAnswer.text: An MBA in Finance is a specialized management program that trains you in financial planning, investment analysis, corporate finance, banking operations, and strategic financial decision-making. It prepares you for leadership roles in the finance and business sector.

mainEntity.name: What skills will I gain through an MBA in Finance?

acceptedAnswer.text: You will develop strong skills in financial analysis, budgeting, risk management, investment planning, portfolio management, forecasting, business valuation, and strategic financial decision-making.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a strong math background to pursue an MBA in Finance?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not necessarily. Basic number skills are sufficient as the program teaches financial concepts from the fundamentals. With consistent practice and guidance, students from any background can excel in finance.

mainEntity.name: What subjects are included in the MBA in Finance curriculum?

acceptedAnswer.text: The curriculum typically covers Corporate Finance, Financial Management, Investment Banking, Financial Markets, Derivatives, Portfolio Management, Risk Management, Strategic Finance, Accounting, and International Finance.

mainEntity.name: What tools or software will I learn in the MBA in Finance program?

acceptedAnswer.text: You will work with essential finance tools like Excel (advanced), financial modeling software, Bloomberg basics, statistical tools, portfolio management platforms, and forecasting tools used in top financial institutions.

mainEntity.name: What career opportunities can I expect after completing an MBA in Finance?

acceptedAnswer.text: MBA Finance graduates can work as Financial Analysts, Investment Bankers, Credit Analysts, Portfolio Managers, Risk Analysts, Corporate Finance Managers, Banking Officers, and Wealth Management Consultants.

mainEntity.name: Which industries hire MBA in Finance professionals?

acceptedAnswer.text: Industries such as banking, financial services, stock markets, insurance, fintech, consulting, real estate, corporate enterprises, and investment firms actively recruit finance professionals.

mainEntity.name: What is the salary outlook after an MBA in Finance?

acceptedAnswer.text: Finance is one of the highest-paying MBA specializations. With expertise in financial modeling, investment analysis, and corporate finance, professionals can quickly grow into well-paid managerial and leadership roles.

mainEntity.name: Does Teksversity provide practical exposure for MBA in Finance students?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Teksversity focuses on real-time learning through financial case studies, portfolio simulations, budgeting exercises, live projects, and industry-oriented financial tools to build strong practical knowledge.

mainEntity.name: Can working professionals pursue an MBA in Finance at Teksversity?

acceptedAnswer.text: Absolutely! Teksversity offers flexible learning options like weekend classes, online programs, and hybrid models, making it ideal for working professionals who want to upgrade their financial career without affecting their job.

MA in Public Policy

overViewImage.alt: MA in Public Policy – Program Overview

overViewOfOnline.subHeading: Master Policy Research, Governance Systems & Strategic Public Leadership
overViewOfOnline.description: The Online MA in Public Policy at Teksversity provides a deep understanding of how public systems operate and how policies shape social, economic and administrative outcomes. The curriculum merges governance studies, public administration, economic policy, development frameworks and research methodologies to cultivate strong analytical and leadership skills. Through real-world simulations, policy labs, case-based learning, and socio-economic assessments, learners develop the ability to design, evaluate and implement impactful policies. This program prepares graduates for influential careers across government institutions, public sector bodies, NGOs, consulting firms, international agencies and development organizations.
keyPoints: Master key concepts in governance and public policy
Build skills in policy design and evaluation
Use tools for data-driven policy decisions
Strengthen leadership and public problem-solving
Prepare for roles in governance and policy research

faq.question: What is an MA in Public Policy?
faq.answer: An MA in Public Policy is a postgraduate program that focuses on understanding, analyzing, and designing policies that address social, economic, and political issues. It trains students to evaluate public problems and create effective solutions for government, NGOs, and private sectors.

faq.question: Who should pursue an MA in Public Policy?
faq.answer: This course is ideal for students interested in governance, social impact, public administration, law, economics, international relations, and development studies. It also suits working professionals in government, NGOs, and corporate social responsibility roles.

faq.question: What skills will I gain from this program?
faq.answer: Students learn policy analysis, economic reasoning, data-driven decision-making, program evaluation, leadership, communication, public finance, and research methods. They also develop strong critical thinking and problem-solving skills.

faq.question: What subjects are covered in the MA in Public Policy curriculum?
faq.answer: Typical subjects include Public Policy Analysis, Economics for Policy, Governance & Institutions, Public Administration, Data Analytics, Development Policy, Research Methods, Public Finance, and Law & Policy.

faq.question: What career opportunities are available after an MA in Public Policy?
faq.answer: Graduates can work as Policy Analysts, Research Associates, Public Affairs Specialists, Program Managers, Development Consultants, Government Officers, CSR Executives, and Data Analysts in national and international organizations.

faq.question: Is an MA in Public Policy suitable for non-political science students?
faq.answer: Yes. Students from any background—engineering, commerce, arts, management, or science—can pursue it. The program starts with foundational courses that help students understand policy concepts easily.

faq.question: What are the eligibility criteria?
faq.answer: Applicants must hold a bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognized university. Some institutions may require entrance exams, interviews, or work experience.

faq.question: Which industries hire Public Policy graduates?
faq.answer: Public Policy graduates are hired by government ministries, international NGOs, think tanks, research organizations, consulting firms, UN agencies, media houses, CSR departments, and development bodies.

faq.question: What is the scope of Public Policy in India?
faq.answer: Public Policy is rapidly growing in India due to increasing demand for evidence-based policymaking in governance, digital transformation, healthcare, education, sustainability, and economic development.

faq.question: Can I pursue higher studies after an MA in Public Policy?
faq.answer: Yes. Students can pursue PhD in Public Policy, Public Administration, Development Studies, Economics, Political Science, or International Relations.
